title: Efficient LLM Inference: Bandwidth, Compute, Synchronization, and Capacity are all you need
content_excerpt:
10
LLM Serving. LLM inference can be broadly divided into two stages: the prefill phase and the decode phase. The prefill phase processes the initial prompt or context, generating intermediate representations of the prompt tokens that are used in the subsequent decode phase. The decode phase, which is auto-regressive, generates one token at a time, conditioned on the previously generated tokens (Figure[1](https://arxiv.org/html/2507.14397v1#S2.F1 "Figure 1 ‣ 2 Modeling ‣ Efficient LLM Inference: Bandwidth, Compute, Synchronization, and Capacity are all you need")shows a tranformer layer for Llama3-70B). In modern deployment scenarios, it is common to have a separate prefill server or cluster and a decode server[10.5555/3691938.3691949](https://arxiv.org/html/2507.14397v1#bib.bib51);[10609649](https://arxiv.org/html/2507.14397v1#bib.bib27), to optimize for each phase. DeepSeekV3’s inference deployment provisions10×10\times10 ×more nodes for decode compared to prefill.Since the decode phase dominates the execution time for many applications, it is the primary focus of this limit study.
| Based on Blackwell GPU (HBM3e) ≤ GPU ModelingNeuSight provides a way to forecast future GPUs’ performance using current GPUs[neusight](https://arxiv.org/html/2507.14397v1#bib.bib20).[habitat](https://arxiv.org/html/2507.14397v1#bib.bib11)and a linear regression based approach[linearregression](https://arxiv.org/html/2507.14397v1#bib.bib23)estimate the latency of GPUs for deep learning.LIMINALis different in goals and the level of modeling - we focus on LLM inference with a goal of exploring vastly different chips. Other analytical modeling approaches, include Paleo[paleo](https://arxiv.org/html/2507.14397v1#bib.bib30), Calculon[calculon](https://arxiv.org/html/2507.14397v1#bib.bib17), and MAD-Max[madmax](https://arxiv.org/html/2507.14397v1#bib.bib15)which decompose latency into computation and communication for distributed training. CPU offloading and efficiencyTechniques like ZeRo, DeepSpeed[10.5555/3433701.3433727](https://arxiv.org/html/2507.14397v1#bib.bib33);[deepspeed2020](https://arxiv.org/html/2507.14397v1#bib.bib24)and PaRO[zhang2024rethinking](https://arxiv.org/html/2507.14397v1#bib.bib49)use innovative techniques to balance capacity and bandwidth for training by streaming through PCIe from host-memory to device memory constantly. Those techniques don’t apply here and simply reduce the memory bandwidth to the PCIe bandwidth if using host memory for serving the decode phase. FlexGen describes techniques for extreme model serving with a single GPU[10.5555/3618408.3619696](https://arxiv.org/html/2507.14397v1#bib.bib38).

LIMINALis meant to be a first order model and is not meant to be cycle-accurate. However, it is a valid question to ask how accurate it is. Here we briefly look at how closeLIMINALis to SOTA GPU hardware. We do this validation in two ways.
